profile.name: Rick Arko
profile.headline: AI/ML Consultant, Founder, and Applied AI Builder
profile.short_tagline: I help founders and operating teams turn AI ideas into reliable products, decision systems, and production-ready workflows.
profile.availability: Available for consulting, fractional AI leadership, and founder collaborations.

hero.headline: Ship AI products that create leverage, not prototypes that die in a demo.
hero.subheadline: I work with founders, operators, and product teams to build practical AI systems across LLMs, forecasting, pricing, recommendation, and automation. The focus is always the same: clear business outcomes, clean implementation, and production reliability.
highlights: LLM product strategy, RAG, evaluation, and workflow automation
Forecasting, pricing, recommendation, and operational decision systems
Fractional AI leadership for teams that need both strategy and execution

stats.label: years building applied ML systems in production
experience across logistics, e-commerce, fintech, and operations
builder mindset from prototype architecture through deployment

about: I am an AI and machine learning consultant with a hands-on background in forecasting, pricing, recommendation systems, and production data products. I have spent the last decade building systems that have to work in messy, high-stakes environments where reliability matters more than novelty.

Today I help teams navigate the shift from classic ML to modern AI. That can mean shaping an AI roadmap, designing a grounded LLM workflow, standing up evaluation and observability, improving an existing model stack, or helping a founding team turn an idea into a focused, shippable product.

My style is pragmatic, technical, and business-aware. I like working closely with decision-makers, shipping quickly, and building systems that are understandable enough to maintain once the first launch excitement wears off.

services.title: AI Strategy and Roadmaps
services.description: Clarify where AI fits, which use cases deserve investment, what the architecture should look like, and how to sequence execution without overcommitting too early.

services.title: LLM Products and Workflow Automation
services.description: Design grounded assistants, retrieval workflows, internal knowledge tools, and process automation that respect latency, cost, security, and trust.

services.title: Applied ML Systems
services.description: Build or improve forecasting, pricing, recommendation, scoring, and optimization systems with an emphasis on measurable business impact.

services.title: Fractional AI Leadership
services.description: Partner with founders or product leaders on technical direction, vendor evaluation, hiring, team mentoring, and go-to-production execution.

expertise.title: LLM Systems
expertise.description: Retrieval-augmented generation, prompt workflows, source-grounded answers, evaluation loops, and internal copilots.

expertise.title: Production Machine Learning
expertise.description: Forecasting, regression, classification, recommendation, anomaly detection, and experimentation in real operating environments.

expertise.title: Decision Infrastructure
expertise.description: Pricing services, operational planning tools, automation pipelines, and data products that have to support live business decisions.

expertise.title: Technical Translation
expertise.description: Bridging founders, executives, operators, analysts, and engineers so that complex systems are usable, trusted, and aligned with the business.

wins.title: Scaled network forecasting
wins.impact: Improved site-level error from 30%+ to under 15% by replacing hundreds of bespoke models with a more scalable ML approach.
wins.summary: Built forecasting systems that could survive constant network change, new locations, new product launches, and noisy operational data.

wins.title: Made pricing models more usable
wins.impact: Matched ensemble-level performance using only five common shipment features, making live pricing services far easier to operationalize.
wins.summary: Focused on practical model design, latency, interpretability, and business continuity in environments where downtime is expensive.

wins.title: Led through ambiguity
wins.impact: Helped grow teams, mentor junior talent, and translate technical work into decisions that stakeholders could actually act on.
wins.summary: Strong projects need more than model quality; they need communication, trust, and systems people can operate.

engagements.title: Founder Sprint
engagements.description: A focused engagement for early-stage teams exploring AI product ideas, architecture, or MVP scoping.

engagements.title: Fractional AI Partner
engagements.description: Ongoing support for teams that need senior technical judgment without a full-time AI executive hire.

engagements.title: Build With Your Team
engagements.description: Hands-on implementation, review, and coaching alongside product, engineering, or analytics teams.

insights.title: Topics I like writing and speaking about
insights.description: I am especially interested in the practical edge where ambitious AI ideas meet messy systems, real users, and actual business constraints.
topics: How to decide whether a use case needs an LLM, classic ML, or better product design
What makes RAG systems feel trustworthy instead of fragile
Evaluation patterns for AI features that touch real customer workflows
Why forecasting and operational ML still matter in the age of generative AI
How founders can avoid expensive AI platform detours early on
